title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Atherosclerosis is a life-threatening disease that may have begun to develop during childhood.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: This condition is a process in which deposits of fatty material, called plaque, build

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: up inside the walls of arteries, reducing or completely blocking blood flow.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Although the exact causes of atherosclerosis are not clear, many scientists think it begins

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: with damage to the inner wall of an artery, called the endothelium, substances traveling

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: in the blood, such as cholesterol, fats, and cellular waste products accumulate inside

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: the damaged area of the arterial wall.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Chemical reactions occurring within the build up of material cause cholesterol molecules

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: to oxidize.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: This initiates an inflammatory response in which the endothelial cells, at the damage site,

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: release chemicals that signal a call for help.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: In response, monocytes from the bloodstream travel to the damaged site.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Stimulation from oxidized cholesterol converts the monocytes into macrophages.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: The macrophages eat and digest the cholesterol molecules.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: As a result of this process, the macrophages change into foam cells, which accumulate

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: to form plaque.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: As the plaque increases in size, the arterial wall thickens and hardens.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: At the same time, smooth muscle cells within the arterial wall begin to multiply.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Most of the smooth muscle cells move to the surface of the plaque.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: These cells contribute to the formation of a firm, fibrous cap covering the plaque.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Eventually, the passageway through the artery narrows, enough to reduce blood flow, and

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: the amount of oxygen received by the organs it supplies.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: Over time, the cap may erode and break open, releasing plaque into the bloodstream.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: The plaque can flow downstream and contribute to the formation of a blood clot, which can

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: stop blood flow.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: As a result, limited blood supply is available to the area surrounding the partially blocked

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
text_en: artery, degrading, and potentially killing the neighboring tissue.

text_en: you

title: ANS00339 Electrocardiography (ECG)
text_en: Sticky pads will be placed on your chest to check your heart rate.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will perform an abdominal aortic aneurysm repair to prevent the rupture of a

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: bulging area of your aorta.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Open abdominal aortic aneurysm repair.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will begin by making an incision from the breastbone to below the navel.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: He or she will then locate the aneurysm and clamp the aorta just above and below it.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will then open the aneurysm and sew an artificial graft the same size and

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: shape as a healthy aorta into place.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will stitch the wall of the aneurysm closed over the newly placed graft.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: The graft will reinforce the walls of the aorta.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: Your surgeon will close the abdominal incision with stitches.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
text_en: The total length of this procedure is usually 4 to 6 hours.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
text_en: The largest artery in the body is the aorta, which is attached to the heart and receives blood directly from the left ventricle or main pumping chamber.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
text_en: The aorta is divided into four parts, the ascending aorta, aortic arch, thoracic aorta, and the abdominal aorta.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
text_en: The abdominal aorta is the last and largest section of the aorta.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
text_en: It supplies blood to the lower regions of your body, including the organs and the abdomen and pelvis.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
text_en: The abdominal aorta ends around naval level, where it splits into the two iliac arteries that carry blood to the legs.
